kubejs.script.client.scripts.jei.descrip: Used for growing crops without a water source block.
Now you can grow them even in the nether and you won't have to worry about animals trampling your crops!

When turned on, it starts spilling on the ground the inventory of up to two blocks behind it. If you place it in front of a water block (cauldron, water/waterlogged), it will start dripping water particles.
You can use it to pour liquids to and from jars. Since it can extract items from the side of a block it enables some sort of automation not possible with vanilla.
It will also interact with other blocks like hives and concrete powder. Its water color will depend on what's behind it.

Stores 4 buckets or 12 bottles of any vanilla liquids (like honey, milk, lava, potions, soups, dragon beath or xp) as well as fireflies. It functions as a shulker box for liquids or a rudimentary tank. You can now also store cookies and fish! Not compatible with the forge fluid system or with other fluid mods, meant to complement vanilla only.
Emits a redstone signal, depending on the weather. The worse the weather, the stronger the signal.
Place an item on top to have it displayed. Stacking multiple pedestals will turn them into a pillar.
Light source that can be switched off with a redstone signal
Outputs a redstone signal that gets stronger the more the crank is rotated.
Launches any entity on top of it when it is given a redstone signal.
When powered, will rotate any item/entity on top of it.
Right click on the block to get the time in hours. You are able to sleep at 18:00 and Dawn is at 06:00
When powered, will blow entities or items in front of it in the direction it is facing.
Transmits redstone power, just like redstone dust, but connects on all sides.
This making vertical redstone easier and looks cooler too.
Extremely hard block that functions as a chest. Retains inventory when broken.
Place sand in it and it will provide a redstone signal until the sand runs out. Flip and repeat.
Can be found in Raid Dungeon as Stage 5 loot
Can be found in Raid Dungeon as Stage 5 loot

The name of the past still continues to haunt... internal reminders like
"minecraft:mending" persist, a name from the dead continues to appear
like a ghost from the grave. Fame is a curse, when you have fame,
everyone seeks you, people will do anything just to have you, no matter
how cruel or horrifying. Many innocent librarian villagers enslaved all
just to have a chance to acquire a taste of your power. Other
enchantments want your fame, and try to obtain it by naming 'selves
things like "life mending" or "ender mending". You feel responsible for
the things that happened to the villagers and wish to escape. First
changing your mechanics; now having the unique ability to reset an
item's Repair Cost. Something that has never been done before beyond
the likes of a grindstone, a truly handy power. But the players still
see your name, and despite others like Life Mending covering your job,
the players still seek your old repairing capabilities. So you change
your name to Repair, a name representing the ability to reset the
Repair Cost. However the players still seek your former self, and no
matter how you change, your old functionality will always continue to
The tragic tale of the most popular enchantment. Being famous and
powerful is an unescapable curse disguised as a blessing.

kubejs.script.server.scripts.custom.raid: §a✅ Dungeon Raid System Loaded! §fStatistic tracking enabled
§8Use §7/raidstats §8to view your full profile & lifetime stats.
§6✦ §f%s Raid Token%s §6deposited into your Raid Vault. §8(/raidvault)
§5☠ Cursed Chest! §7You have been afflicted with §c%s§7.
§c👁 A guardian emerges from the chest!
§c💀 You died in the dungeon! §f-%s Raid XP §8(%s%% of rank progress)
§c💀 You died in the dungeon! §8(No XP lost — re-enter to continue)
§c✗ You must be inside a dungeon raid to use this.
§c✗ This item only works inside the dungeon dimension.
§c✗ You must be inside a dungeon raid to use this.
§c✗ This item only works inside the dungeon dimension.
§cThis item only hums with power inside a dungeon.
§b🌀 You feel a pull towards the entrance...
§cThis item only hums with power inside a dungeon.
§cThis item only hums with power inside a dungeon.
§7Enter the §6Dungeon Portal§7 with a §fDungeon Pass§7. Kill mobs, open chests, and exit the portal when you're done. Your performance is tracked the entire time.

§7Every kill and chest contributes to a score pool. When you exit, that score converts into §6Raid Tokens§7 deposited directly into your §6Raid Vault§7. Bosses and minibosses are worth more. Higher rank gives a token bonus multiplier.

§7Killing mobs and opening chests earns §bRaid XP§7. Enough XP and you rank up. Higher rank means mobs hit harder and have more health — but your token rewards improve and rival raiders become more common.
§7Dying in the dungeon costs a small portion of your current rank's XP progress. You §lcannot lose a rank§r§7 from dying. Your run is not over — re-enter and keep going.
§7Once per run, a random event fires at random time after entry. It might buff you, curse the mobs, double your score, or make things considerably harder. It lasts about 1 minute.
§7Some chests are cursed. Opening them gives loot as normal, but also applies a random debuff. Effects clear when you exit.
§7/raidvault deposit      §8— deposit held tokens into vault
§8/raidvault deposit  §8— deposit held tokens into vault
§cYou are not holding any Raid Tokens!
§cYou need a %s%s Key §cin your off-hand to open this.
kubejs.script.server.scripts.custom.ritu: The text crawls across the page like ink-black insects.
The parchment is stained with the soot and blood of battle.
Dried leaves and wild vines spill from the broken seal.
Heat radiates from the charred, soot-covered binding.
The pages are damp, smelling of salt and ancient depths.
The binding is hot to the touch, smelling of sulfur.
A cold, crushing pressure emanates from the dark ink.
Small brass gears and springs fall from the pages.
The paper feels lighter than air, shimmering with starlight.
Spores puff out as the seal breaks, smelling of damp earth.
A blast of freezing air escapes as the frost-crusted seal snaps.
The weight of the book is immense; gold flakes peel from its edges.
Static discharge numbs your fingers as you break the binding.
The tome feels as if it wants to float away into the clouds.
Echoes of dripping water and shifting stone emerge from the pages.
The scent of ancient piglin forges and pig-iron fills the air.
The ink glows with the sickly green light of an ender eye.
Pressure builds in your ears, as if you were miles underwater.
Dust and cobwebs spill out; the history here is almost forgotten.
The rhythmic clicking of tiny invisible gears stops as you open it.
The binding is made of sun-bleached bone, cold and brittle.
Chitinous clicking sounds emanate from within the paper.
The leather cover feels like coarse, cold scales.
A thick, iron-scented fluid stains your hands as the seal breaks.
The power within is suffocating; the pages are scorched by dragonfire.
Light seems to get sucked into the open pages, leaving only darkness.
The pages chime like bells as they are turned.
Warmth and blinding light pour from the script.
Whispers fill your mind, urging you to close the book immediately.
Every movement you make is repeated twice in the silence of the tome.
